sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, the player is now above the center-below frame. The center frame (the long horizontal one) is at row 28-31, col 32-47. The center-below frame is at row 36-39, col 32-35. The player is at row 32-35 (white head), col 28-31, and the green body is at row 36, col 28-31.
Wait, looking at the image more carefully, the player's green body seems to be overlapping with the center frame (the long horizontal one). Let me check the segmentation.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print("Player position:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'N']:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

print("\nFrames:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['c', 'G', 'b', 'S']:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The player is at row 29-31 (green body), col 28-31. The white head is at row 24-28, col 28-31. The center-below frame is at row 36-39, col 32-35. The center frame (long horizontal) is at row 28-31, col 32-39.
The player is to the left of the center frame. The center frame is 'b' (blue), not 'c'. The center-below frame is 'c'.
Let me try pressing SPACE when the player is above the center-below frame. But first, I need to move to be above it (same column range).
Actually, the player is at col 28-31, and the center-below frame is at col 32-35. The player is to the left of the frame. Let me move RIGHT to be above the frame.
Wait, the center frame is at row 28-31, col 32-39. The player's white head is at row 24-28, col 28-31. So the player is above and to the left of the center frame.
Let me try moving RIGHT to get above the center-below frame.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move RIGHT to be above the center-below frame
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
